tasks_completed.description: Filled in test_AC1: asserts full marker template and substitution wording present in commands/spec-update.md
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced pytest.fail sentinel with static read of spec-update.md asserting '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <resolved-task-id> -->' template and 'substituting the actual task-id value' wording
tasks_completed.rationale: AC1 verifies the marker instruction exists in spec-update.md; static text check is the correct approach

tasks_completed.description: Filled in test_AC2: asserts full marker template and first-line placement instruction in commands/spec-update.md
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced self-fulfilling temp-file/subprocess approach with static read asserting marker template shape and 'first line of the new cycle block' instruction
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding: original test wrote the marker itself and proved grep could find what it wrote — did not verify spec-update.md at all

tasks_completed.description: Filled in test_AC3: asserts exact prohibition phrase against literal placeholder forms
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced weak independent assertions with exact prohibition phrase check: 'never a literal `${TASK_ID}` or `<task-id>` placeholder'
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding: '<task-id>' appears in artifact path examples unrelated to the marker; exact phrase check is the only non-vacuous test

tasks_completed.description: Filled in test_AC4: asserts all spec-continuation-of occurrences fall within Continuation-spec mode section, with non-vacuous match guard
tasks_completed.changes: Added 'assert matches' guard before boundary check to prevent vacuous pass when zero matches exist
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding: without the guard, a file with no 'spec-continuation-of' text would pass AC4 — the text must exist

tasks_completed.description: Filled in test_AC5: asserts idempotency instruction appears in the marker paragraph with all required components
tasks_completed.changes: Located marker paragraph by index, normalized whitespace to handle line wrapping, asserted 'If it is already present', 'do not write a second copy', and 'exactly once per' all appear in that paragraph
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding: idempotency wording must be tied to the marker instruction context, not checked globally across the file

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — implementation (commands/spec-update.md) was already correct; only test stubs needed filling
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Test stubs were auto-generated with TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels; dev cycle needed to fill them in
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: All 5 stubs now perform real static assertions against commands/spec-update.md content

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 5 independent test files each require distinct assertions; codex review strengthened each from ~8 lines to ~15-25 lines of real assertion logic. All lines are load-bearing test assertions — none are scaffolding or comment filler.

dev.qa_notes: Run: python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260524-125300-C/ -v — all 5 must exit 0. Tests are static and do not require any live spec-update run.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 5 findings all with PROPOSED_FIX: (1) AC2 was self-fulfilling — wrote marker to temp file then proved grep found it; (2) AC1 only checked prefix not full template; (3) AC3 '<task-id>' check too weak — appears in unrelated artifact paths; (4) AC4 could pass vacuously with zero matches; (5) AC5 idempotency wording not tied to marker paragraph. All findings were in-scope real bugs.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 findings applied: AC2 rewritten as static check; AC1 asserts full template + substitution wording; AC3 asserts exact prohibition phrase; AC4 adds assert-matches guard; AC5 finds marker paragraph by index and normalizes whitespace before asserting idempotency sequence.

recommendations: AC3 test relies on exact wording from commands/spec-update.md; if wording is ever edited, test must be updated to match.
